channel.title: Action Adventure Games on Chasing Dings!
channel.description: Recent content in Action Adventure Games on Chasing Dings!

item.title: Odin Sphere: Two dimensions is more than enough
item.description: It’s a 2D side-scrolling action RPG about a brave Valkyrie and her overbearing father, Odin.

item.title: The Hobbit (Gameboy Advance)
item.description: See, it’s a story about a little man who lives in a hole dug into a hill…

item.title: Star Trek Prodigy: Supernova
item.description:
Out of all the new-era Star Trek shows, Star Trek Prodigy is the one that strays furthest from the basic template. It’s the first targeted specifically toward children, and the first that, at least at first, doesn’t involve Starfleet.

It’s Star Trek for a generation that has been raised on the Disney Star Wars animated shows. And so it is likely the first Star Trek or Star Wars show to really ask – and answer – the question, who would win? Star Trek or Star Wars?

item.title: The Amazing Adventures of 8-Ball!
item.description: “Adventure of 8-Ball” is a 2D platformer mobile game with 50 levels, attractive graphics, and catchy music. The player controls 8-ball, a little black cat, on a quest to save their owner from the evil dog catcher who has kidnapped them.

item.title: The Seven Sages: A Masterclass in Metroidvania Design
item.description: In “The Seven Sages”, you play as one of the seven ancient sages of mystery, who must journey through a vast and dangerous world to gather the seven magical artifacts that will save the realm from a great evil.

item.title: Steve, the Fifth Horseman of the Apocalypse
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 5th: “Steve, the Fifth Horseman of the Apocalypse”.

This is a game where you play as Steve, the fifth horseman of the Apocalypse, who was left behind when the other four horsemen rode off to bring about the end of the world. With only your trusty steed and a few weapons, you must now ride across the country, fighting off hordes of zombies and other monsters that have taken over the world.

item.title: Fourt Knight: An Awesome Game That's Worth the Price
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 4th: “Fourt Knight”.

“Fourt Knight” is an action adventure game where you play a lone knight who must save the kingdom from a dark force.

The game is set in a medieval world and you will use your sword and shield to fight your way through hordes of enemies. You will also use your bow to take down enemy archers. There are puzzles to solve and secret areas to explore.
